instruction: Rewrite the content to meet insurance regulatory and compliance standards.

input.draft_text:
What is a Free Look Period?
A life insurance policy is, usually, a long-term contract. You buy the policy for your medium-term and long-term financial goals. Moreover, if financial security and income replacement are the concern, you look for the maximum possible tenure so that you can enjoy coverage for a longer duration.
After buying the life insurance policy, it is recommended that you pay the premiums as and when they are due. Missing premium payments or surrendering the policy should be avoided as they result in reduced benefits. In fact, in a term plan, if you stop premium payments, you might end up losing the premiums previously paid as well as the coverage.
What if you bought the wrong policy or if you are not satisfied with your purchase? Can you do something about it without incurring a considerable loss?
Yes, you can. There’s a concept of a free-look period that helps you cancel the policy without incurring a loss. Let’s understand.
Free Look Period Definition
The free look period is also called the cooling-off period. It is a period during which you can cancel your insurance policy if you are not satisfied with it. Life insurance companies usually allow a free look period of 15 days from the policy issuance date. This period extends to 30 days if you buy the policy through digital marketing channels or for annual modes.
If you cancel the policy within the free look period, the premium that you have paid is refunded back to you after deducting some minor expenses incurred in issuing the coverage.
Why Should You Consider Free Look Period?
You can consider cancelling the policy during the free look period in the following instances –
You bought the policy without understanding the complete terms and conditions. Thereafter, when you find out about the terms and conditions, you do not want to continue the coverage.
Your agent mis-sold the policy
Your needs have changed after buying the policy
You are not happy with the benefits of the plan
You have found another plan offering better coverage at lower premiums
You bought the policy in haste and do not want to continue the coverage
You are having second thoughts
The policy does not fit into your financial requirements
How Does Free Look Period Work?
After your policy is issued, you are allowed the free look period. The period starts from the date the policy is issued and continues up to 15 or 30 days as the case may be. You enjoy the coverage during the free look period.
During this period, if you want to terminate the coverage, you can request the insurance company for the same.
Upon receiving the request for free look cancellation, the insurer would cancel the plan. Your premium would be refunded after adjusting for the following expenses –
The administrative costs incurred in issuing the policy,
Stamp duty of the plan,
Cost of medical check-ups, if done before issuance,
Cost of insurance cover provided up to the date the policy is cancelled calculated proportionately, and
Any other costs that the company incurred in issuing the policy1.
After the premium is refunded, the coverage would be cancelled.
Essential Details Related to Free Look Period
Some of the important aspects of the free look period, that you should know, are as follows –
You would have to inform the insurer to cancel the policy during the free look period. This intimation can be given online or offline. You would have to fill up a cancellation form for the same.
The free look period is available in health insurance plans too. However, the duration might differ.
The insurer might require you to submit some documents when you opt for the free look cancellation.
In the case of ULIPs, when you cancel the plan during the free look period, the insurance company might repurchase the allocated units at a specified rate.
Every life insurance policy offers a free look period as per the directions issued by the Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India (IRDAI).
If you cancel the policy during the free look period, the tax deduction under Section 80C, for the premium, would also be reversed.
So, understand what the free look period is all about, its duration and the rights that it gives you. You can cancel the insurance policy during the free look period for a premium refund. However, once the free look period has passed, cancelling the policy would result in reduced benefits and should be avoided.
Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)
What would happen if I cancel the policy after the free look period?
Once the free look period has passed, the cancellation would prove challenging. If you cancel or surrender, you would get a very low fraction of the premium as surrender value.
Do you have to provide a reason for the cancellation of the policy?
Yes, insurance companies ask about the reason for cancellation. They try and help you get a resolution for the reason for which you are cancelling the policy. However, if there is no resolution, the cancellation is allowed and the premium is refunded back.
Does free-look cancellation incur any charge?
No, free look cancellation is completely free of cost. However, the cost incurred on providing the coverage and issuing the policy would be deducted from the premium before its refund.
Disclaimer:
The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. For more details on risk factors, terms and conditions please read the sales brochure & policy document (available on www.bajajallianzlife.com) carefully before concluding a sale. output.final_text:
What is a Free Look Period?
A life insurance policy is, usually, a long-term contract. You may buy the policy for your medium- term and long-term financial protection goals. Moreover, if financial security and income replacement are the concerns, you look for the maximum possible tenure so that you can enjoy coverage for a longer duration.
What if you bought the wrong policy or if you are not satisfied with your purchase? Can you do something about it without incurring a considerable loss?
Yes, you can. There’s a concept of a free-look period that helps you cancel the policy without incurring a loss, subject to nominal charges. Let’s understand.
Free Look Period Definition
The free look period is also called the cooling-off period. It is a period during which you may cancel your insurance policy if you are not satisfied with the terms and conditions of the same. Life insurance companies usually allow a free look period of 15 days from the date of receipt of the policy bond by the policyholder to review the terms and conditions of the policy. This period extends to 30 days if you buy electronic policies and policies sourced through distance mode.
If you cancel the policy within the free look period, the premium that you have paid is refunded back to you after deducting some minor expenses incurred by the insurer in issuing the coverage.
Purpose of providing Free Look Period
The intent is to provide an option of cancelling the policy during the free look period in the following instances –
● You bought the policy without understanding the complete terms and conditions. Thereafter, when you receive the policy bond and find out about the terms and conditions, you do not want to continue with the coverage. ● Your needs have changed after buying the policy, you may want to opt for a different sum assured or term ● You are not satisfied with the benefits of the plan ● You have found another plan offering suitable coverage at more affordable premiums ● You bought the policy in haste and do not want to continue with the coverage ● You are having second thoughts ● The policy no more fit into your financial requirements
How Does Free Look Period Work?
After your policy is issued, you are allowed a free look period. The period starts from the date of receipt of the policy bond by the policyholder and continues up to 15 day and 30 days in case of electronic policies and policies sourced through distance mode.
During this period, if you want to terminate the coverage, you can request the insurance company for the same.
Upon receiving the request for free look cancellation, the insurer would cancel the plan. Your premium would be refunded, if you have not made any claim during the free look period after adjusting for the following expenses –
● any expenses incurred by the insurer on medical examination of the insured persons and the stamp duty charges or; ● where the risk has already commenced and the option of return of the policy is exercised by the policyholder, a deduction towards the proportionate risk premium for period during which the cover was active or; ● Where only a part of the insurance coverage has commenced, such proportionate premium commensurate with the insurance coverage during such period; ● In respect of Unit Linked Policy, in addition to the above deductions, the insurer shall also be entitled to repurchase the unit at the price of the units as on the date of the return of the policy. Once the policy is cancelled pursuant to the free look cancellation request, the coverage would be cancelled.
Essential Details Related to Free Look Period
Some of the important aspects of the free look period, that you should know, are as follows –
● You would have to inform the insurer to cancel the policy during the free look period. This intimation can be given online or offline. You would have to fill up a cancellation form for the same. ● The insurer might require you to submit some documents when you opt for the free look cancellation. ● In the case of ULIPs, when you cancel the plan during the free look period, the insurance company is entitled to repurchase the allocated units at the price of the units on the date of cancellation. ● Every life insurance policy offers a free look period as per the directions issued by the Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India (IRDAI). So, understand what the free look period is all about, its duration and the rights that it gives you. You can cancel the insurance policy during the free look period for a premium refund subject to deduction of applicable charges.
Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)
1. What would happen if I cancel the policy after the free look period?
Once the free look period has passed, and you choose to surrender the policy, you would get a very low fraction of the premium as surrender value depending on terms and conditions of the policy. 2. Do you have to provide a reason for the cancellation of the policy?
Yes, insurance companies ask about the reason for cancellation. They try and help you get a resolution for the reason for which you are cancelling the policy. However, if there is no resolution, the cancellation is allowed and the premium is refunded back subject to deduction of applicable charges. 3. Does free-look cancellation incur any charge?
The insurer may deduct the cost incurred on providing pro-rated risk coverage, medical tests, stamp duty, etc. from the premium before processing the refund.
Disclaimer:
